calendar.matters: MASTER CALENDAR COURT
MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE

Motion To Compel Response To Discovery Requests And
For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF SIPING ZHONG'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY REQUESTS AND FOR SANCTIONS.

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion to compel discovery responses is denied. Though the motion is not opposed, the motion papers do not include the required separate statement or the interrogatory responses asserted to be deficient. Nor do the motion papers show that there were sufficient meet and confer efforts. Moreover, while it is unclear, it appears that the motion may be untimely since it seeks to compel further responses more than 45 days after service of the assertedly deficient responses. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

Off Calendar Jun-20-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT YONG LAN DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to (1) clearly identify which cause of action is alleged against which defendant; (2) to allege facts (not conclusions) in support of each element of each cause of action alleged against Defendant Lan. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
